content: Antibiotic resistance is a growing concern that affects us all, regardless of our industry or expertise. The rise of antibiotic-resistant bacteria threatens not only public health but also the global economy, with estimated losses in the trillions of dollars. When antibiotics fail, patients require longer hospital stays, more expensive treatments, and may even die from infections that were once easily treatable. Antibiotic resistance occurs when bacteria evolve to withstand the effects of antibiotics, often due to the overuse or misuse of these medications. The widespread use of antibiotics in agriculture and the lack of new antibiotic developments have contributed significantly to the spread of resistance. To combat this, a multi-faceted approach is needed, including responsible antibiotic use in healthcare and agriculture, and investment in new treatments. Companies can support research, promote sustainable agriculture, and advocate for policies to curb antibiotic misuse. By working together, we can address this critical issue and ensure the long-term health and prosperity of our communities and economies.
